_comment: Committed reference records for the AstryxOS FF-headless perf benchmark. Curated, travels with the branch, reviewed in PRs. The full rolling per-run history lives host-locally at ~/.astryx-perf/timeseries.jsonl and is NOT committed. Record schema is documented in scripts/perf-bench.py (schema_v=1, additive-only — never rename a field). These two records are REFERENCE TARGETS, not live measurements: the dashboard surfaces them as a reference band, and the per-revision rolling series is compared against them. Replace/augment them with clean measurements via the MEASUREMENT RUNBOOK once a quiet host is available.

records.short_desc: AstryxOS render-to-PNG reference (median of the 6 historical PNG-success runs)
records.note: Median of the 6 historical render-to-PNG runs (revisions be0730f, 631beed, f96ae94, 5055220). total_tick_ms is the kernel-tick recoverable total (~30 min — the headline problem this benchmark tracks). This is a target band, NOT a clean measurement; regenerate per the runbook on a quiet host.

records.short_desc: Linux KVM baseline — Alpine 3.20 upstream firefox-esr (placeholder; fill via perf-baseline-linux.py run)
records.note: The 'should-be' Linux reference: the SAME upstream musl firefox-esr renders /tmp/hello.html to /tmp/out.png on a stock Alpine 3.20 KVM guest, typically in single-digit seconds. ff_exec_to_png_ms is THE comparable figure (FF-runtime window, host clock). Fill via: python3 scripts/perf-baseline-linux.py run --i-understand-this-boots (after acquire-image --do-download).
